thesis: ASP Isotopes is an advanced materials company producing enriched isotopes for nuclear medicine, quantum computing, green energy, and semiconductors. The January 2026 Renergen acquisition adds helium and LNG assets, while the planned QLE spin-off targets HALEU and lithium isotopes. Q1 2026 revenue surged 280% YoY to $4.2M. High short interest (23% of float) and analyst consensus price target of $13+ create asymmetric upside on execution.

drivers: QLE spin-off unlocks value and focuses core isotope business
Silicon-28 commercial shipments scale for quantum computing
Renergen Phase 1 completion positions ASPI as major helium producer
HALEU contracts secured from advanced reactor developers
High short interest creates squeeze potential on positive catalysts

risks: Commercial delays in isotope shipments and Renergen ramp
Cash burn of ~$25M/quarter forces dilutive capital raise
Technology scale-up proves more costly than projected

base_case.assumptions: Revenue ramps to $50–80M by 2027 as commercial contracts materialize. Renergen contributes steady cash flow by 2027–2028. QLE spin-off completes at modest valuation.

catalysts.event: Renergen Virginia Gas Project Phase 1 completion

key_risks: Pre-profitability with high cash burn rate
Dilution risk from future equity raises
Regulatory and licensing delays for nuclear materials
South Africa geopolitical and infrastructure risks (Renergen)
